key_insight: Static equilibrium notions like Nash, correlated, and coarse correlated equilibria can hide destabilizing dynamics, leading to misleading or unbounded price-of-anarchy guarantees in multi-agent learning.
engineering_relevance: For distributed control, traffic routing, and other multi-agent systems, the paper warns that performance guarantees based only on equilibrium analysis may fail under realistic learning dynamics.
method: Show that interior Nash equilibria can lack useful local vector-field information for distinguishing incentives.
Analyze how pure and coarse equilibria can correspond to unstable, dominated, or chaotic dynamic states.
Study congestion games and non-atomic limits to reveal destabilization, repellers, and exponential inefficiency under discrete-time learning.
evidence_signal: The abstract cites theoretical analysis across congestion games and learning dynamics, including unbounded PoA cases, Li-Yorke chaos, and asymptotic bounds such as Θ(p/ln p) and 2^p degradation.
watch_item: These are worst-case theoretical results, so translating them into practical design rules requires care about model assumptions, update rules, and game structure.
paper_title: Paradoxes of Game Theoretic Equilibria and Price of Anarchy
